board.location: first floor airing cupboard, alongside hot-water cylinder bulkhead

circuit_label.text: C-B1 | Bathroom luminaires | L1 | RCBO 6A B-curve | 1.5mm² | 30mA TpA — §701
circuit_label.svg: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="50mm" height="12mm" viewBox="0 0 50 12" version="1.1"><title>Circuit label per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 sect514 - Way W1</title><desc>Per-circuit strip-label for UK bathroom luminaire circuit.</desc><rect x="0" y="0" width="50" height="12" fill="#FFFFFF" stroke="#000000" stroke-width="0.2"/><text x="2" y="3.5" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="2.5" font-weight="bold" fill="#000000">W1</text><text x="8" y="3.5" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="2.5" fill="#000000">Bathroom luminaires</text><text x="2" y="7" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="2" fill="#000000">L1 | RCBO 6A | 1.5mm2</text><text x="2" y="10.5" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="2" fill="#000000">30mA TpA RCD per sect701</text></svg>

diversity_basis.citation: IET OSG App A + BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 311.1 — continuous lighting load (bathroom luminaires + extract fan run together), no diversity

circuits.designation: Bathroom shaver socket (BS EN 61558-2-5) + shower-pump spur

circuit_label.text: C-B2 | Bath shaver + shower-pump spur | L1 | RCBO 16A B-curve | 2.5mm² | 30mA TpA — §701
circuit_label.svg: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no"?><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="50mm" height="12mm" viewBox="0 0 50 12" version="1.1"><title>Circuit label per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 sect514 - Way W2</title><desc>Per-circuit strip-label for UK bathroom shaver + shower-pump circuit.</desc><rect x="0" y="0" width="50" height="12" fill="#FFFFFF" stroke="#000000" stroke-width="0.2"/><text x="2" y="3.5" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="2.5" font-weight="bold" fill="#000000">W2</text><text x="8" y="3.5" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="2.5" fill="#000000">Bath shaver + pump</text><text x="2" y="7" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="2" fill="#000000">L1 | RCBO 16A | 2.5mm2</text><text x="2" y="10.5" font-family="Arial, sans-serif" font-size="2" fill="#000000">30mA TpA RCD per sect701</text></svg>

diversity_basis.citation: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 § 311.1 — engineer-declared mixed-load (shaver supply unit ~50 VA + shower pump ~1500 W); coincident demand assumed at 1.00 (worst case both running) per § 311.1 individual-load assessment

non_compliance_flags.message: Distribution scope cut — DB-FF1 actually carries 9 circuits total; only the 2 bathroom-serving circuits (C-B1, C-B2) are shown for the Part-7 cascade demonstration. The 7 first-floor non-bathroom circuits (C-L01..C-L05 + C-S01..C-S03) are omitted from this example and remain on the deferred-deliverable list.

assumptions: Both C-B1 + C-B2 are RCBO-protected per circuit (30 mA Type A) — preferred topology over a grouped main RCD for bathroom-serving circuits per BS 7671 § 314.1 (avoidance of unwanted disconnection).
Cable ampacity check assumes BS 7671 Appendix 4 Reference Method C (clipped direct).
Selectivity verification deferred — fault-level intent absent; engineer-declared Ifault 1.5 kA recorded; cascade pairs marked tool_call_pending per WI3.

Bathroom served by this board is the UK 2700×2100 mm fixture from sprint examples D.2 (lighting-layout) and D.4 (small-power) — circuits C-B1 and C-B2 here correspond to the upstream consumers in those drawings.

zones._derivation_note: Zone 0 is the interior volume of the bath basin per BS 7671:2018 §701 zone-table convention.
Zone 1 above the bath polygon, from rim level (550mm) to 2250mm AFF per §701 zone-table convention.
Shower-over-bath: Zone 1 from shower head at 2100mm AFF down to floor; coincides with bath polygon. Overlap with zone_bath_1_z1 declared per INV-01.
Zone 2 = 600mm horizontal extension of Zone 1 polygon per §701 zone-table; unclipped polygon shown for derivation transparency.
Zone 2 from shower_1: 600mm horizontal extension of Zone 1; ceiling at shower head (2100mm). Coincides with bath_1 zone_2 (shower-over-bath). Overlap with zone_bath_1_z2 declared per INV-01.

electrical_constraints._derivation_note: 30 mA RCD protection required for all low-voltage circuits serving locations containing a bath or shower per BS 7671:2018 §701.411.3.3. Heater exemption does not apply here — bathroom, not sauna.

rationale.chat_summary: First-floor consumer unit DB-FF1 — 230 V single-phase 60 A switch-disconnector main, IP2X Form 1 enclosure, 10-way Hager-style. Scope cut shows only the 2 bathroom-serving circuits (C-B1 luminaires 6 A RCBO + C-B2 shaver/shower-pump 16 A RCBO) so the special-locations cascade can be exercised in isolation. Both circuits sit downstream of a 30 mA Type A RCBO each — preferred topology over grouped main RCD per § 314.1. The cascaded §701 rcd_blanket constraint is satisfied; INV-16 PASS. Reply to refine (e.g. add the other 7 first-floor circuits, swap to grouped main RCD).

sections.title: Board Classification
sections.summary: UK first-floor consumer unit per BS EN 61439-3 DBO Form 1, IP2X for airing cupboard install.

decisions.label: Form 1 DBO enclosure
decisions.summary: Standard for domestic CUs at this rating — no internal separation required.

sections.title: Incoming Supply
sections.summary: 230 V single-phase 60 A switch-disconnector from PME service (Ze 0.35 Ω declared). 60 A (rather than the 100 A used on a full first-floor CU) reflects the scope-cut nature of this example: only 2 circuits modelled here, ~3.5 kVA design demand on the bathroom feeds.

decisions.label: 60 A switch-disconnector main
decisions.summary: Sufficient for the modelled 2-circuit scope; sized 1.5× design demand for headroom.
decisions.rule: DNO service rating + assembly main sizing

sections.title: Way Assignment
sections.summary: 2 ways used (W1, W2), 8 spare. Meets engineer-specified minimum 4. Spare-way count is generous because 7 non-bathroom circuits (omitted from this example) would seat on W3..W9 in the full deliverable.

sections.title: OCPD Selection
sections.summary: Both circuits are Type B RCBOs — combined OCPD + 30 mA Type A RCD on a single module. Preferred over grouped main RCD per BS 7671 § 314.1 (avoidance of unwanted disconnection): a fault on C-B1 must not also trip C-B2 and leave the occupant unable to disable the shower pump or use the shaver socket.

decisions.label: Per-circuit RCBO topology
decisions.summary: Each bathroom circuit gets its own combined OCPD+RCD module — selectivity-by-circuit at the residual-current level.

decisions.label: Type B curve
decisions.summary: Domestic loads (bathroom lighting + shaver supply unit + shower pump) have no significant inrush — Type B trips earliest on low-magnitude shorts.

sections.title: Special-Locations Cascade (Part 7)
sections.summary: Cascade source: electrical/special-locations/examples/cascade-db-layout-uk-bathroom-rcd-enforcement/intent-out.json. Payload contributes 5 zones (Z0/Z1 bath + Z1 shower-over-bath + Z2 bath + Z2 shower-over-bath) and 1 electrical_constraint (rcd_blanket_by_room, 30 mA, applies to lighting + sockets + shower_unit + fixed_equipment + shaver_socket). Distribution-side response: both C-B1 (lighting) and C-B2 (shaver + shower pump) are 30 mA Type A RCBO-protected — satisfies the blanket constraint.

decisions.label: RCD blanket satisfied via per-circuit RCBO
decisions.summary: 30 mA Type A on each bathroom-serving circuit. Sauna-heater exemption does not apply (room is a bathroom, not a sauna).

decisions.label: No additional Part-7 distribution rules fire
decisions.summary: This is a domestic bathroom, not a Medical (§710), Pool (§702), or Sauna (§703). So: no Medical IT panel (§710 family), no pool main-bonding terminal (§702 family), no supplementary bonding terminal required at DB-FF1 (§701 supplementary bonding moved local to the room per amendment 2; not a board-side requirement).
decisions.rule: Negative-coverage check — Part-7 distribution rules NOT engaged here

sections.title: Cable Selection
sections.summary: C-B1: 1.5 mm² T&E, Iz_C ≈ 19.5 A at 30 °C; In = 6 A — comfortable margin. C-B2: 2.5 mm² T&E, Iz_C ≈ 27 A at 30 °C; In = 16 A — comfortable margin. Both routes under 20 m so volt-drop trivially within 3 % for the modelled loads.

sections.title: Selectivity Verification
sections.summary: 2 cascade pairs (MAIN → C-B1, MAIN → C-B2) emitted as tool_call_pending. fault-level intent absent; engineer-declared Ifault = 1.5 kA recorded on incoming_supply.declared_pfc_ka but full selectivity verification deferred to runtime.

sections.title: Compliance
sections.summary: Compliant. One INFO flag noting the example scope cut (2 of 9 actual circuits modelled). Cascade flag part7_zone_present set; INV-16 PASS; all 16 INVs PASS or N/A.

invariants.evidence: Way accounting: ways_total=10, 2 circuits seated (W1, W2), 8 spare; sum balances (2 + 8 = 10). Specialty-board branch not engaged (board_kind='main_switchboard', consumer-unit-style domestic CU).

Busbar rating coverage: 60 A main + busbar with IcW=10 kA per BS EN 61439-3. Modelled load on L1 is 8.7 A (lighting 1.74 A + mixed-load circuit 6.96 A at 230 V / 1.6 kW × 1.00). Selectivity emitted as tool_call_pending; will re-verify once fault-level intent lands.

OCPD-cable coordination per BS 7671 § 433.1.1: C-B1 In=6 A ≤ Iz=19.5 A (1.5 mm² T&E at 30 °C ref method C). C-B2 In=16 A ≤ Iz=27 A (2.5 mm² T&E at 30 °C ref method C). Both circuits pass outright with comfortable margin.

INV-14 board DB-FF1 (GB → BS): R1 jurisdiction→format mapping (GB/KE → BS 7671 § 514) holds; R2 board_label.text+svg populated, no {{}} remnants; R3 board_label.text matches BS format regex; R4 2/2 circuits carry circuit_label.text+svg, no {{}} remnants; R5 every circuit_label.text matches BS circuit regex; R6 tool_call_pending booleans set on all labels.

INV-15 board DB-FF1 (GB): both 2 circuits carry diversity_basis. load_types: lighting_continuous×1, other_declared×1 — all valid enum (Rule 1 PASS). factors: 1.0×2. Citations follow BS 7671 / IET OSG family — both ≥20 chars with recognisable clause markers (Reg / §) (Rule 2 PASS). Rule 3 N/A (no lift/ev_charger). Rule 4 N/A (no largest_plus_remainder_pct circuits — vacuous PASS).

INV-16 cascade source: electrical/special-locations/examples/cascade-db-layout-uk-bathroom-rcd-enforcement/intent-out.json. Sub-check 1 PASS (consumed_intents.special_locations_zoning present + payload.compliant=true). Sub-check 2 PASS (5 zones + 1 constraint count matches payload). Sub-check 3 PASS — circuits walked: C-B1 (lighting, 30 mA Type A RCBO) + C-B2 (shaver + shower pump mixed, 30 mA Type A RCBO); both gain RCD protection per rcd_blanket_by_room rule. Sub-check 4 PASS — distribution sub-rules: (a) 30 mA RCD applied to all 2 circuits serving the bathroom; (b) no Medical IT panel required (room is § 701 bathroom not § 710 medical); (c) no pool main-bonding terminal required (no § 702 swimming pool); (d) no supplementary bonding terminal at DB-FF1 required for § 701 bathroom (supplementary bonding moved local to room per amendment 2). Only the rcd_blanket rule fires for this room class.
